# Dodam

> A gentle fortnightly letter on balcony gardening and growing at a humane pace

## Pages

- [Dodam](http://localhost:48731/): A gentle fortnightly letter on balcony gardening and growing at a humane pace
- [About](http://localhost:48731/about/): Why Dodam exists, who writes it, and what to expect every other Sunday

## Issues

- [Herbs That Forgive You](http://localhost:48731/issues/herbs-that-forgive-you/): Mint, perilla, and chives will survive almost anything you do wrong on a balcony — which makes them the right place to begin, or to recover.
- [On Overwatering as an Act of Anxiety](http://localhost:48731/issues/overwatering-as-an-act-of-anxiety/): Overwatering is rarely about the plant needing water. It is usually about the gardener needing to do something.
- [Repotting in the Heat](http://localhost:48731/issues/repotting-in-the-heat/): Late spring root-bound plants and early-summer heat arrive at the same time. Here is how to repot without shocking either the plant or yourself.
- [The Fig Tree's Second Spring](http://localhost:48731/issues/the-fig-trees-second-spring/): A rooted fig cutting that did almost nothing its first year finally budded out this spring. Two years, it turns out, was always the honest timeline.
- [The Tomato That Took Its Time](http://localhost:48731/issues/the-tomato-that-took-its-time/): One cherry tomato seedling lagged three weeks behind its siblings. Rushing it would have cost more than waiting did.
- [What a Balcony Actually Needs](http://localhost:48731/issues/what-a-balcony-actually-needs/): Before buying anything else, a real accounting of what a small balcony garden requires to begin — and what it does not.
